links.title: Hydra: Unifying Document Retrieval and Generation in a Single Vision-Language Model

primary_link.title: Hydra: Unifying Document Retrieval and Generation in a Single Vision-Language Model

papers.summary: Visual document understanding typically requires separate retrieval and generation models, doubling memory and system complexity. We present Hydra, a dual-head approach that provides both ColBERT-style late-interaction retrieval and autoregressive generation from a single vision-language model (VLM). A single LoRA adapter, trained only for retrieval, is toggled at inference: enabling it produces multi-vector embeddings; disabling it recovers the base model's generation quality -- byte-identical
papers.summary_zh: 視覺文件理解通常需要獨立的 retrieval 和 generation 模型，這會使記憶體和系統複雜度倍增。我們提出了 Hydra，這是一種雙頭方法，可從單一 vision-language model (VLM) 提供 ColBERT-style 的 late-interaction retrieval 和 autoregressive generation。在 inference 時，一個專為 retrieval 訓練的單一 LoRA adapter 會被切換：啟用它會產生 multi-vector embeddings；禁用它則會恢復 base model 的 generation 品質——達到 byte-identical。

links.title: ResAdapt: Adaptive Resolution for Efficient Multimodal Reasoning

primary_link.title: ResAdapt: Adaptive Resolution for Efficient Multimodal Reasoning

papers.summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve stronger visual understanding by scaling input fidelity, yet the resulting visual token growth makes jointly sustaining high spatial resolution and long temporal context prohibitive. We argue that the bottleneck lies not in how post-encoding representations are compressed but in the volume of pixels the encoder receives, and address it with ResAdapt, an Input-side adaptation framework that learns how much visual budget each frame should receive before encoding. ResAdapt couples a lightweight Allocator with an unchanged MLLM backbone, so the backbone retains its native visual-token interface while receiving an operator-transformed input. We formulate allocation as a contextual bandit and train the Allocator with Cost-Aware Policy Optimization (CAPO), which converts sparse rollout feedback into a stable accuracy-cost learning signal. Across budget-controlled video QA, temporal grounding, and image reasoning tasks, ResAdapt improves low-budget operating points and often lies on or near the efficiency-accuracy frontier, with the clearest gains on reasoning-intensive benchmarks under aggressive compression. Notably, ResAdapt supports up to 16x more frames at the same visual budget while delivering over 15% performance gain. Code is available at https://github.com/Xnhyacinth/ResAdapt.
papers.summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型 (MLLMs) 透過提高輸入保真度實現更強大的視覺理解，但由此產生的視覺 token 增長使得同時維持高空間解析度 (spatial resolution) 和長時間上下文 (temporal context) 變得難以承受。我們認為瓶頸不在於編碼後表示的壓縮方式，而在於編碼器接收的像素量，並透過 ResAdapt 解決此問題。ResAdapt 是一個輸入端 (Input-side) 自適應框架，它學習每個幀在編碼前應接收多少視覺預算 (visual budget)。ResAdapt 將輕量級 Allocator 與未更改的 MLLM backbone 耦合，因此 backbone 保持其原生的 visual-token 介面，同時接收經過運算子轉換的輸入。我們將分配問題 формуulate 為一個 contextual bandit，並使用 Cost-Aware Policy Optimization (CAPO) 訓練 Allocator，該優化將稀疏的 rollout feedback 轉換為穩定的精度-成本學習信號。在預算控制的視訊問答 (video QA)、時間定位 (temporal grounding) 和圖像推理 (image reasoning) 任務中，ResAdapt 改善了低預算操作點，並且通常位於效率-精度曲線 (efficiency-accuracy frontier) 上或附近，在積極壓縮下，對推理密集型基準測試的增益最為明顯。值得注意的是，ResAdapt 在相同的視覺預算下支援多達 16 倍的幀數，同時提供超過 15% 的性能提升。程式碼可在 https://github.com/Xnhyacinth/ResAdapt 取得。
papers.title: ResAdapt: Adaptive Resolution for Efficient Multimodal Reasoning
papers.title_zh: ResAdapt：用於高效多模態推理的自適應解析度

papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 日益增長的 agentic capabilities 已使其能夠部署於真實世界應用，例如雲端服務。在這些應用中，客戶與助理的互動呈現高技術複雜度和長程依賴性 (long-horizon dependencies)，使得穩健性 (robustness) 和解決效率 (resolution efficiency) 對於客戶滿意度至關重要。然而，現有針對 LLM-based agents 的基準測試大多依賴於合成環境，未能捕捉真實客戶的多元性和不可預測性。

links.title: Superintelligence and Law

primary_link.title: Superintelligence and Law

papers.summary: The prospect of artificial superintelligence -- AI agents that can generally outperform humans in cognitive tasks and economically valuable activities -- will transform the legal order as we know it. Operating autonomously or under only limited human oversight, AI agents will assume a growing range of roles in the legal system. First, in making consequential decisions and taking real-world actions, AI agents will become de facto subjects of law. Second, to cooperate and compete with other actors (human or non-human), AI agents will harness conventional legal instruments and institutions such as contracts and courts, becoming consumers of law. Third, to the extent AI agents perform the functions of writing, interpreting, and administering law, they will become producers and enforcers of law. These developments, whenever they ultimately occur, will call into question fundamental assumptions in legal theory and doctrine, especially to the extent they ground the legitimacy of legal institutions in their human origins. Attempts to align AI agents with extant human law will also face new challenges as AI agents will not only be a primary target of law, but a core user of law and contributor to law. To contend with the advent of superintelligence, lawmakers -- new and old -- will need to be clear-eyed, recognizing both the opportunity to shape legal institutions as society braces for superintelligence and the reality that, in the longer run, this may be a joint human-AI endeavor.
papers.summary_zh: 人工超級智慧（即在認知任務和具有經濟價值的活動中普遍超越人類的 AI agents）的前景，將徹底改變我們所知的法律秩序。AI agents 將自主運作或在有限的人類監督下，在法律體系中扮演越來越多的角色。首先，在做出具有重大影響的決策和採取實際行動時，AI agents 將成為事實上的法律主體。其次，為了與其他參與者（人類或非人類）合作和競爭，AI agents 將利用傳統的法律工具和機構，例如 contracts 和 courts，成為法律的消費者。第三，如果 AI agents 執行法律的撰寫、解釋和管理功能，它們將成為法律的生產者和執行者。這些發展，無論何時最終發生，都將質疑法律理論和學說中的基本假設，特別是當這些假設將法律機構的合法性建立在其人類起源之上時。試圖使 AI agents 與現有的人類法律保持一致，也將面臨新的挑戰，因為 AI agents 不僅是法律的主要規範對象，也將是法律的核心使用者和貢獻者。為了應對超級智慧的到來，立法者——無論新舊——都需要保持清醒的頭腦，既要認識到在社會應對超級智慧之際塑造法律機構的機會，也要認識到從長遠來看，這可能是一項人類與 AI 的共同努力。

links.title: GEditBench v2: A Human-Aligned Benchmark for General Image Editing

primary_link.title: GEditBench v2: A Human-Aligned Benchmark for General Image Editing

papers.summary: Recent advances in image editing have enabled models to handle complex instructions with impressive realism. However, existing evaluation frameworks lag behind: current benchmarks suffer from narrow task coverage, while standard metrics fail to adequately capture visual consistency, i.e., the preservation of identity, structure and semantic coherence between edited and original images. To address these limitations, we introduce GEditBench v2, a comprehensive benchmark with 1,200 real-world user queries spanning 23 tasks, including a dedicated open-set category for unconstrained, out-of-distribution editing instructions beyond predefined tasks. Furthermore, we propose PVC-Judge, an open-source pairwise assessment model for visual consistency, trained via two novel region-decoupled preference data synthesis pipelines. Besides, we construct VCReward-Bench using expert-annotated preference pairs to assess the alignment of PVC-Judge with human judgments on visual consistency evaluation. Experiments show that our PVC-Judge achieves state-of-the-art evaluation performance among open-source models and even surpasses GPT-5.1 on average. Finally, by benchmarking 16 frontier editing models, we show that GEditBench v2 enables more human-aligned evaluation, revealing critical limitations of current models, and providing a reliable foundation for advancing precise image editing.

links.title: DreamLite: A Lightweight On-Device Unified Model for Image Generation and Editing

primary_link.title: DreamLite: A Lightweight On-Device Unified Model for Image Generation and Editing

papers.summary: Diffusion models have made significant progress in both text-to-image (T2I) generation and text-guided image editing. However, these models are typically built with billions of parameters, leading to high latency and increased deployment challenges. While on-device diffusion models improve efficiency, they largely focus on T2I generation and lack support for image editing. In this paper, we propose DreamLite, a compact unified on-device diffusion model (0.39B) that supports both T2I generation and text-guided image editing within a single network. DreamLite is built on a pruned mobile U-Net backbone and unifies conditioning through in-context spatial concatenation in the latent space. It concatenates images horizontally as input, using a (target | blank) configuration for generation tasks and (target | source) for editing tasks. To stabilize the training of this compact model, we introduce a task-progressive joint pretraining strategy that sequentially targets T2I, editing, and joint tasks. After high-quality SFT and reinforcement learning, DreamLite achieves GenEval (0.72) for image generation and ImgEdit (4.11) for image editing, outperforming existing on-device models and remaining competitive with several server-side models. By employing step distillation, we further reduce denoising processing to just 4 steps, enabling our DreamLite could generate or edit a 1024 x 1024 image in less than 1s on a Xiaomi 14 smartphone. To the best of our knowledge, DreamLite is the first unified on-device diffusion model that supports both image generation and image editing.

links.title: Kernel-Smith: A Unified Recipe for Evolutionary Kernel Optimization

primary_link.title: Kernel-Smith: A Unified Recipe for Evolutionary Kernel Optimization

top5.summary: We present Kernel-Smith, a framework for high-performance GPU kernel and operator generation that combines a stable evaluation-driven evolutionary agent with an evolution-oriented post-training recipe. On the agent side, Kernel-Smith maintains a population of executable candidates and iteratively improves them using an archive of top-performing and diverse programs together with structured execution feedback on compilation, correctness, and speedup. To make this search reliable, we build backend-specific evaluation services for Triton on NVIDIA GPUs and Maca on MetaX GPUs. On the training side, we convert long-horizon evolution trajectories into step-centric supervision and reinforcement learning signals by retaining correctness-preserving, high-gain revisions, so that the model is optimized as a strong local improver inside the evolutionary loop rather than as a one-shot generator. Under a unified evolutionary protocol, Kernel-Smith-235B-RL achieves state-of-the-art overall performance on KernelBench with Nvidia Triton backend, attaining the best average speedup ratio and outperforming frontier proprietary models including Gemini-3.0-pro and Claude-4.6-opus. We further validate the framework on the MetaX MACA backend, where our Kernel-Smith-MACA-30B surpasses large-scale counterparts such as DeepSeek-V3.2-think and Qwen3-235B-2507-think, highlighting potential for seamless adaptation across heterogeneous platforms. Beyond benchmark results, the same workflow produces upstream contributions to production systems including SGLang and LMDeploy, demonstrating that LLM-driven kernel optimization can transfer from controlled evaluation to practical deployment.
top5.summary_zh: 我們提出 Kernel-Smith，這是一個用於高效能 GPU kernel 和 operator 生成的框架，它結合了穩定的評估驅動型演化 agent 和以演化為導向的後訓練方案。在 agent 端，Kernel-Smith 維護一個可執行候選集合，並利用頂級效能和多樣化程式的歸檔，結合編譯、正確性和加速方面的結構化執行回饋，迭代地改進它們。為了使這種搜尋可靠，我們為 NVIDIA GPU 上的 Triton 和 MetaX GPU 上的 Maca 建構了後端特定的評估服務。在訓練端，我們透過保留保持正確性、高增益的修訂，將長時程演化軌跡轉換為以步驟為中心的監督和 reinforcement learning 訊號，從而使模型在演化迴圈內部作為一個強大的局部改進者進行最佳化，而不是作為一個一次性生成器。在統一的演化協定下，Kernel-Smith-235B-RL 在 KernelBench 上使用 Nvidia Triton 後端達到了最先進的整體效能，取得了最佳的平均加速比，並超越了包括 Gemini-3.0-pro 和 Claude-4.6-opus 在內的頂級專有模型。我們進一步在 MetaX MACA 後端驗證了該框架，我們的 Kernel-Smith-MACA-30B 超越了 DeepSeek-V3.2-think 和 Qwen3-235B-2507-think 等大型模型，突顯了在異構平台之間無縫適應的潛力。除了基準測試結果，相同的工作流程還為 SGLang 和 LMDeploy 等生產系統貢獻了上游內容，證明了 LLM 驅動的 kernel 最佳化可以從受控評估轉移到實際部署。
top5.title: Kernel-Smith: A Unified Recipe for Evolutionary Kernel Optimization
top5.title_zh: Kernel-Smith：一種用於演化核心最佳化的統一方案

links.title: Gen-Searcher: Reinforcing Agentic Search for Image Generation

primary_link.title: Gen-Searcher: Reinforcing Agentic Search for Image Generation

top5.summary: Recent image generation models have shown strong capabilities in generating high-fidelity and photorealistic images. However, they are fundamentally constrained by frozen internal knowledge, thus often failing on real-world scenarios that are knowledge-intensive or require up-to-date information. In this paper, we present Gen-Searcher, as the first attempt to train a search-augmented image generation agent, which performs multi-hop reasoning and search to collect the textual knowledge and reference images needed for grounded generation. To achieve this, we construct a tailored data pipeline and curate two high-quality datasets, Gen-Searcher-SFT-10k and Gen-Searcher-RL-6k, containing diverse search-intensive prompts and corresponding ground-truth synthesis images. We further introduce KnowGen, a comprehensive benchmark that explicitly requires search-grounded external knowledge for image generation and evaluates models from multiple dimensions. Based on these resources, we train Gen-Searcher with SFT followed by agentic reinforcement learning with dual reward feedback, which combines text-based and image-based rewards to provide more stable and informative learning signals for GRPO training. Experiments show that Gen-Searcher brings substantial gains, improving Qwen-Image by around 16 points on KnowGen and 15 points on WISE. We hope this work can serve as an open foundation for search agents in image generation, and we fully open-source our data, models, and code.
top5.summary_zh: 最近的圖像生成模型在生成高保真和寫實圖像方面展現出強大的能力。然而，它們根本上受限於凍結的內部知識，因此常常在知識密集型或需要最新資訊的真實世界場景中失效。在本文中，我們提出 Gen-Searcher，作為首次嘗試訓練一種搜尋增強型圖像生成 agent，它執行多跳推理和搜尋以收集 grounded generation 所需的文本知識和參考圖像。為此，我們建構了一個量身定制的資料流程，並策劃了兩個高品質資料集，Gen-Searcher-SFT-10k 和 Gen-Searcher-RL-6k，其中包含多樣化的搜尋密集型 prompts 和相應的 ground-truth 合成圖像。我們進一步介紹了 KnowGen，這是一個綜合基準，明確要求用於圖像生成的搜尋 grounded 外部知識，並從多個維度評估模型。基於這些資源，我們使用 SFT 訓練 Gen-Searcher，然後進行 agentic reinforcement learning，並結合雙重獎勵回饋，將基於文本和基於圖像的獎勵結合起來，為 GRPO 訓練提供更穩定和資訊豐富的學習訊號。實驗表明，Gen-Searcher 帶來了顯著的收益，在 KnowGen 上將 Qwen-Image 提高了約 16 點，在 WISE 上提高了 15 點。我們希望這項工作能為圖像生成中的搜尋 agents 提供一個開放的基礎，並且我們完全開源了我們的資料、模型和程式碼。
top5.title: Gen-Searcher: Reinforcing Agentic Search for Image Generation
top5.title_zh: Gen-Searcher：強化圖像生成中的智能體式搜尋

links.title: HISA: Efficient Hierarchical Indexing for Fine-Grained Sparse Attention

primary_link.title: HISA: Efficient Hierarchical Indexing for Fine-Grained Sparse Attention

top5.summary: Token-level sparse attention mechanisms, exemplified by DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA), achieve fine-grained key selection by scoring every historical token for each query using a lightweight indexer, and then computing attention only over the selected subset. While the downstream sparse attention scales efficiently, the indexer still scans the entire prefix for every query, introducing an O(L^2) per-layer bottleneck that becomes prohibitive as context length grows. We propose HISA (Hierarchical Indexed Sparse Attention), a drop-in replacement for the indexer that transforms the search process from a flat token scan into a two-stage hierarchical procedure. First, a block-level coarse filter scores pooled block representatives to prune irrelevant regions. Then, a token-level refinement applies the original indexer only within the remaining candidate blocks. HISA preserves the exact token-level top-k sparsity pattern required by the downstream Sparse MLA operator and requires no additional training. On kernel-level benchmarks, HISA achieves a 2times speedup at 32K context length and 4times at 128K. On Needle-in-a-Haystack and LongBench, we directly replace the indexer in DeepSeek-V3.2 with HISA, without any fine-tuning. HISA closely matches the original DSA in quality while significantly outperforming block-sparse baselines. Moreover, the token selection sets produced by HISA and the original DSA exhibit a mean IoU greater than 99%, indicating that the efficiency gains come with virtually no impact on selection fidelity.
top5.summary_zh: 以 DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) 為例的 token-level 稀疏注意力機制，透過使用輕量級 indexer 為每個查詢評分每個歷史 token，然後僅對選定的子集計算注意力，從而實現細粒度的 key 選擇。雖然下游的稀疏注意力可以高效地擴展，但 indexer 仍然為每個查詢掃描整個前綴，引入了 O(L^2) 的每層瓶頸，這在上下文長度增加時變得難以承受。我們提出 HISA (Hierarchical Indexed Sparse Attention)，它是 indexer 的直接替換，將搜尋過程從平面 token 掃描轉換為兩階段的階層式程序。首先，block-level 的粗略過濾器對 pooled block representatives 進行評分以修剪不相關的區域。然後，token-level 的精煉僅在剩餘的候選 blocks 內應用原始的 indexer。HISA 保留了下游 Sparse MLA operator 所需的精確 token-level top-k 稀疏模式，並且無需額外訓練。在 kernel-level 基準測試中，HISA 在 32K 上下文長度下實現了 2 倍的加速，在 128K 下達到了 4 倍。在 Needle-in-a-Haystack 和 LongBench 上，我們直接將 DeepSeek-V3.2 中的 indexer 替換為 HISA，無需任何 fine-tuning。HISA 在品質上與原始 DSA 密切匹配，同時顯著優於 block-sparse 基準。此外，HISA 和原始 DSA 產生的 token 選擇集合的平均 IoU 大於 99%，這表明效率提升幾乎不影響選擇的 fidelity。
top5.title: HISA: Efficient Hierarchical Indexing for Fine-Grained Sparse Attention
top5.title_zh: HISA：高效能階層式索引，實現細粒度稀疏注意力

links.title: Marco DeepResearch: Unlocking Efficient Deep Research Agents via Verification-Centric Design

primary_link.title: Marco DeepResearch: Unlocking Efficient Deep Research Agents via Verification-Centric Design

top5.summary: Deep research agents autonomously conduct open-ended investigations, integrating complex information retrieval with multi-step reasoning across diverse sources to solve real-world problems. To sustain this capability on long-horizon tasks, reliable verification is critical during both training and inference. A major bottleneck in existing paradigms stems from the lack of explicit verification mechanisms in QA data synthesis, trajectory construction, and test-time scaling. Errors introduced at each stage propagate downstream and degrade the overall agent performance. To address this, we present Marco DeepResearch, a deep research agent optimized with a verification-centric framework design at three levels: (1)~QA Data Synthesis: We introduce verification mechanisms to graph-based and agent-based QA synthesis to control question difficulty while ensuring answers are unique and correct; (2)~Trajectory Construction: We design a verification-driven trajectory synthesis method that injects explicit verification patterns into training trajectories; and (3)~Test-time scaling: We use Marco DeepResearch itself as a verifier at inference time and effectively improve performance on challenging questions. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our proposed Marco DeepResearch agent significantly outperforms 8B-scale deep research agents on most challenging benchmarks, such as BrowseComp and BrowseComp-ZH. Crucially, under a maximum budget of 600 tool calls, Marco DeepResearch even surpasses or approaches several 30B-scale agents, like Tongyi DeepResearch-30B.
top5.summary_zh: 深度研究 agents 自主進行開放式調查，整合複雜的資訊檢索與多步驟推理，跨越多樣來源以解決真實世界問題。為了在長時程任務中維持這種能力，在訓練和推斷過程中，可靠的驗證至關重要。現有範式的一個主要瓶頸源於 QA 資料合成、軌跡建構和測試時擴展中缺乏明確的驗證機制。每個階段引入的錯誤會向下傳播並降低 agent 的整體效能。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 Marco DeepResearch，這是一個深度研究 agent，透過以驗證為中心的框架設計在三個層次進行最佳化：(1)~QA 資料合成：我們將驗證機制引入基於圖和基於 agent 的 QA 合成中，以控制問題難度，同時確保答案是獨特且正確的；(2)~軌跡建構：我們設計了一種驗證驅動的軌跡合成方法，將明確的驗證模式注入訓練軌跡中；以及 (3)~測試時擴展：我們在推斷時使用 Marco DeepResearch 本身作為驗證器，並有效提高在挑戰性問題上的效能。大量的實驗結果表明，我們提出的 Marco DeepResearch agent 在大多數挑戰性基準測試（例如 BrowseComp 和 BrowseComp-ZH）上顯著優於 8B 規模的深度研究 agents。關鍵的是，在最大 600 次工具調用的預算下，Marco DeepResearch 甚至超越或接近了多個 30B 規模的 agents，例如 Tongyi DeepResearch-30B。
top5.title: Marco DeepResearch: Unlocking Efficient Deep Research Agents via Verification-Centric Design
top5.title_zh: Marco DeepResearch：透過以驗證為中心的設計，釋放高效能深度研究智能體

links.title: Seeing with You: Perception-Reasoning Coevolution for Multimodal Reasoning

primary_link.title: Seeing with You: Perception-Reasoning Coevolution for Multimodal Reasoning

top5.summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has substantially enhanced the reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). However, existing RLVR approaches typically rely on outcome-driven optimization that updates both perception and reasoning using a shared reward based solely on the final answer. This shared reward blurs credit assignment, frequently improving reasoning patterns while failing to reliably enhance the accuracy of upstream visual evidence extractio
top5.summary_zh: 帶有可驗證獎勵的 reinforcement learning (RLVR) 大幅提升了 multimodal large language models (MLLMs) 的推理能力。然而，現有的 RLVR 方法通常依賴於結果驅動型最佳化，該最佳化僅基於最終答案的共享獎勵來更新感知和推理。這種共享獎勵模糊了信用分配，頻繁地改進推理模式，但未能可靠地提升上游視覺證據提取的準確性。
top5.title: Seeing with You: Perception-Reasoning Coevolution for Multimodal Reasoning
top5.title_zh: 與你共視：多模態推理中的感知-推理協同演化
